evidence: WordSketchApiServer imports 6 inner result classes from SemanticFieldExplorer (ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExploreOptions) — the API layer is structurally coupled to the query layer's internal DTO namespace (WordSketchApiServer.java lines 13-21)

handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti (WordSketchApiServer ~line 563) reimplements seed-collocate intersection logic inline (null-init + retainAll loop) instead of delegating to SemanticFieldExplorer.compare() or a dedicated multi-seed method; the semanticFieldExplorer field is present but unused in that handler

ExploreOptions record carries an 'executor' field that is always passed as null from all three call sites in WordSketchApiServer; callers must know to pass null, making the boundary contract misleading

Response-building is performed inline in every handler via raw Map<String, Object> construction, with no shared mapper/assembler; the same rounding idiom (Math.round(x * 100.0) / 100.0) appears ~10 times across two handlers
QueryExecutor interface is a clean seam: all query operations flow through a declared contract, allowing both test injection and production use without leaking BlackLab types to callers

strengths: QueryExecutor interface provides a clean, typed boundary between the api and query layers; BlackLab types never cross it
HttpApiUtils concentrates all response/CORS/method-guard logic in one place; handlers call it uniformly
BlackLabQueryExecutor delegates all XML parsing to BlackLabSnippetParser, keeping its own code focused on query orchestration
mid_level_elegance.issue_character: The seam issues are concentrated at one boundary — the api-to-query handoff around SemanticFieldExplorer — where result DTOs leak outward as inner class imports, the multi-seed handler bypasses the explorer entirely with inline orchestration, and an unused ExploreOptions field silently misleads callers.
mid_level_elegance.score_rationale: The QueryExecutor/HttpApiUtils seams are genuinely well-designed and hold up across the codebase. However, the SEF boundary has three distinct problems: inner-class DTO leakage, bypassed orchestration in the multi-seed handler, and a misleading contract field (ExploreOptions.executor always null). These are not isolated rough edges — they represent the primary integration seam for the semantic-field feature, which is the newest and most prominent subsystem. That pulls the score into the 60-70 range: a developer navigating this seam encounters awkward implicit contracts and parallel implementations that undermine confidence in the module boundary.

issues.summary: API layer imports 6 inner result classes from SemanticFieldExplorer, coupling api to query internals
evidence: WordSketchApiServer lines 13-21 import SemanticFieldExplorer.ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ExploreOptions as named inner classes
SemanticFieldExplorer javadoc (line 55) acknowledges these should move to a dedicated model/ package
issues.suggestion: Move ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExploreOptions, and Edge into a new pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.model package. Update imports in WordSketchApiServer and SemanticFieldExplorer. This severs the api→query inner-class dependency.

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti reimplements collocate intersection inline, bypassing SemanticFieldExplorer
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti iterates seeds manually, calls executor.executeSurfacePattern per seed, and computes intersection via commonCollocates.retainAll — all inline (~lines 620-650)
SemanticFieldExplorer.compare() (line 293 of SemanticFieldExplorer) already provides multi-noun comparison with richer output; the multi-seed handler ignores it
semanticFieldExplorer field is available in the handler's class but is not called in handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti
issues.suggestion: Add a SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreMulti(Set<String> seeds, String relationName, ...) method that encapsulates seed-loop and intersection logic. Have handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti delegate to it, mirroring how handleSemanticFieldExplore delegates to exploreByPattern.

issues.summary: ExploreOptions.executor field is always passed null by all call sites, creating a misleading boundary contract
evidence: Both ExploreOptions construction sites in WordSketchApiServer pass null for the executor parameter (lines ~503 and ~638)
ExploreOptions is a record with an 'executor' field; a caller reading the signature cannot know it is always null in production use
issues.suggestion: Remove the executor field from ExploreOptions if it is always null in practice. If it was intended for override injection, document the expected non-null contract and add a null-guard or assertion in exploreByPattern.

root_causes: SemanticFieldExplorer grew result DTOs as inner classes before a dedicated model layer was established; the API layer had to import them directly
Multi-seed feature was added to the API layer without routing through the existing explorer abstraction, creating a parallel implementation path
